risks.title: Persistent Inflationary Pressures and Monetary Policy Tightening
risks.summary: Elevated inflation, driven by factors like AI capex spillovers, energy prices, and supply chain disruptions, is expected to remain sticky. This could force central banks to maintain or even tighten monetary policy, potentially slowing economic growth.
risks.watch_for: Sequential core PCE inflation readings near 3% or higher, and any indication of unmooring inflation expectations.
sources: Guggenheim Investments (July 2026 Economic Outlook and Key Themes)
Wells Fargo Investment Institute (2026 Midyear Outlook: Keeping Discipline in Noisy Markets, June 17 2026)
KPMG (Economic Compass - Spring thaw, summer heat, June 10 2026)
JPMorgan (Mid-year market outlook 2026, June 12 2026)
Federal Reserve (Monetary Policy Report – July 2026)

risks.title: Geopolitical Instability and Trade Disruptions
risks.summary: Ongoing geopolitical conflicts, such as the Middle East conflict, are driving energy price increases and commodity market disruptions. Escalating hostilities and new geopolitical strains pose significant downside risks to global growth and trade.
risks.watch_for: Further escalation of the Middle East conflict, and any new major geopolitical flashpoints.
sources: World Bank (Global Economic Prospects - June 2026)
KPMG (Economic Compass - Spring thaw, summer heat, June 10 2026)
Wells Fargo Investment Institute (2026 Midyear Outlook: Keeping Discipline in Noisy Markets, June 17 2026)
JPMorgan (Mid-year market outlook 2026, June 12 2026)

risks.title: US Fiscal Deficits and Rising Debt Levels
risks.summary: US federal debt is projected to reach a record 120% of GDP by 2036, driven by increasing deficits. Rising interest costs on this debt could strain government finances and crowd out other spending.
risks.watch_for: CBO's updated projections on debt and deficit levels, and any legislative actions related to fiscal policy.
sources: Congressional Budget Office (The Budget and Economic Outlook: 2026 to 2036, February 11 2026)
KPMG (The two faces of the economy, January 12 2026)

risks.title: AI Investment Boom and Productivity Lag
risks.summary: The significant capital expenditures in AI are driving business investment, but the expected boost to productivity growth is taking time to materialize. This could lead to inflationary pressures in the short term before delivering long-term benefits.
risks.watch_for: The pace of AI capital spending versus the realization of productivity gains.
sources: Guggenheim Investments (July 2026 Economic Outlook and Key Themes)
KPMG (Economic Compass - Spring thaw, summer heat, June 10 2026)
Stanford SIEPR (The U.S. economy in 2026: What to watch for, March 13 2026)
Wells Fargo Investment Institute (2026 Midyear Outlook: Keeping Discipline in Noisy Markets, June 17 2026)

risks.title: Labor Market Stabilization and Wage Growth Concerns
risks.summary: While the labor market shows signs of stabilization with steady unemployment rates, concerns remain about the pace of wage growth and its potential impact on inflation. Immigration curbs and retirements are also limiting labor supply.
risks.watch_for: Changes in payroll growth, unemployment rate trends, and wage inflation data.
sources: Stanford SIEPR (The U.S. economy in 2026: What to watch for, March 13 2026)
Guggenheim Investments (July 2026 Economic Outlook and Key Themes)
KPMG (Economic Compass - Spring thaw, summer heat, June 10 2026)
Federal Reserve (Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee, June 16-17, 2026)

risks.title: Erosion of Trust and Policy Uncertainty
risks.summary: A decline in trust, both domestically and globally, coupled with political uncertainty and potential leadership transitions in key institutions, can create headwinds for investment and economic activity.
risks.watch_for: Developments in political landscapes, regulatory changes, and leadership announcements at central banks.
sources: KPMG (Economic Compass - Navigating an erosion in trust, February 13 2026)
Wells Fargo Investment Institute (2026 Midyear Outlook: Keeping Discipline in Noisy Markets, June 17 2026)
Guggenheim Investments (Macro Themes for 2026, January 20 2026)

risks.title: Energy Price Volatility and Supply Chain Disruptions
risks.summary: The conflict in the Middle East has led to sharp increases in energy prices and disruptions to global supply chains. Persistent high oil prices remain a key risk, eroding disposable income and impacting consumer sentiment.
risks.watch_for: Developments in the Middle East conflict, and their impact on global oil production and shipping costs.
sources: World Bank (Global Economic Prospects - June 2026)
KPMG (Economic Compass - Running on Empty?, April 08 2026)
Wells Fargo Investment Institute (2026 Midyear Outlook: Keeping Discipline in Noisy Markets, June 17 2026)

risks.title: Interest Rate Uncertainty and Fed Policy Path
risks.summary: The path of future interest rate adjustments by the Federal Reserve remains a key uncertainty. While some anticipate rate cuts, others foresee potential hikes depending on inflation and labor market data.
risks.watch_for: FOMC statements, Summary of Economic Projections (SEP), and Federal Reserve officials' public remarks.
sources: Federal Reserve (FOMC Statement: March 2026, March 19 2026)
Wells Fargo Investment Institute (2026 Outlook report: Trendlines over headlines, December 10 2025)
Guggenheim Investments (July 2026 Economic Outlook and Key Themes)
JPMorgan (2026 mid-year outlook: Resilient growth, sticky inflation, shifting policy, June 29 2026)

consensus_note: There is a broad consensus among institutions regarding the persistent nature of inflation and the significant downside risks posed by geopolitical instability and trade disruptions. While AI investment is seen as a growth driver, its productivity benefits are expected to materialize over time, with near-term inflationary implications. Divergence exists in the precise timing and magnitude of expected monetary policy shifts, with some institutions anticipating rate cuts while others remain cautious due to sticky inflation.
